pi_items.source: Task instruction explicitly requests this folder, and export_result.sh explicitly searches for 'trainingexamples' case-insensitively.
Task instruction requests 5 distinct spam emails. The verifier.py logic explicitly tests count >= min_examples (which defaults to 5).
Task instruction explicitly requests this recipient. The verifier.py logic extracts target_recipient from metadata to score the email.
Task instruction requests at least 3 categories. The verifier.py logic hardcodes the requirement 'if len(categories_found) >= 3:'.
The verifier.py logic imports this dictionary directly from task.json metadata to match keywords in the drafted email body.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real email data from the SpamAssassin corpus (50 ham, 20 spam). The target folder 'Training-Examples' is expected to contain at least 5 spam emails. The announcement email must be addressed to 'all-staff@company.com' and its content must include keywords matching at least 3 distinct phishing technique categories (deception, urgency, commercial, financial, technical) defined by the verifier logic.
